name: Point Breeze
description_short: Maryland-based credit union offering personal and business banking, auto loans, mortgages, and credit cards to eligible members with competitive rates and community focus.
description_long:
Point Breeze Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative based in Maryland that has been serving personal and small business customers. The organization is currently undergoing a merger with Central Credit Union of Maryland, as prominently noted across their website. As a credit union, Point Breeze operates under a not-for-profit structure designed to return value directly to members rather than shareholders.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, mortgages, home equity lines of credit, and business banking services. They provide digital banking capabilities through online banking, mobile banking, bill pay, digital wallet, and phone/text banking. Their loan portfolio spans vehicle financing (auto, boat, motorcycle, RV), personal loans, student loans, business lines of credit, and home lending products including refinancing options.

Point Breeze distinguishes itself through membership benefits and community commitment. The credit union reported saving members $16.1 million in direct financial benefits in the prior year (per CUNA Membership Benefits Report, December 2024). They offer money-saving discounts on insurance and services, scholarships worth $2,000 each to eligible high school seniors, and claim to provide better rates and fewer fees than traditional banks. The organization emphasizes financial education through their blog, virtual workshops, and Zogo financial education tools.

As a credit union, Point Breeze requires membership eligibility, which they note can be achieved through various pathways including employer affiliation and geographic location. The pending merger with Central Credit Union of Maryland represents a significant organizational change that may affect service delivery and product offerings. Like all credit unions, membership requirements and field of membership restrictions may limit accessibility compared to traditional banks.
services: Free Checking and Savings Accounts with competitive APY
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) up to 3.80% APY
Auto Loans as low as 4.49% APR
Personal Loans and Lines of Credit
Home Equity Lines of Credit and Home Equity Loans
Business Banking (checking, savings, CDs, loans, credit cards)
Online Banking, Mobile Banking, and Bill Pay
pros: Reports $16.1 million in member savings benefits annually per CUNA data
Offers auto loans as low as 4.49% APR with streamlined application process
Home Equity Lines of Credit with 2.49% introductory APR for 12 months
CDs earning up to 3.80% APY and Free Checking PLUS+ earning up to 3.00% APY
Provides $2,000 scholarships to eligible members' high school seniors
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile app, digital wallet, and early deposit features
Member discount programs on car insurance, home insurance, and mobile services
cons: Membership eligibility restrictions apply—not accessible to all consumers like traditional banks
Pending merger with Central Credit Union of Maryland creates uncertainty about future operations and service continuity
Limited physical footprint as regional Maryland-based credit union with fewer locations than major national banks
Credit card APR starts at 12.50%, which is higher than promotional rates offered by some card issuers
Website heavily promotes merger information with repeated notices, suggesting transition in progress that may affect service stability
best_for: Maryland residents and eligible employees seeking competitive auto loans and mortgage rates with member-owned benefits
Small business owners needing business checking, savings, loans, and credit card services with personalized service
Members prioritizing community impact and financial education through scholarships and workshops
Consumers seeking home equity financing with low introductory rates for debt consolidation
diagnosis: Point Breeze Credit Union is best for Maryland residents and eligible employees seeking competitive rates on auto loans, mortgages, and credit cards with the added benefit of member ownership and community commitment. The primary caveat is the pending merger with Central Credit Union of Maryland, which creates operational uncertainty, and membership eligibility restrictions that limit accessibility compared to traditional banks.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
